description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the ayufan/openshift-cli Docker image and an OPENSHIFT_SERVER variable, with stages test, review, staging, production, and cleanup. Add test1 and test2 jobs in the test stage that run `echo run tests`. Add a production job in the production stage that runs a shared before_script (`oc login "$OPENSHIFT_SERVER" --token="$OPENSHIFT_TOKEN" --insecure-skip-tls-verify` and `oc project "$CI_PROJECT_NAME-$CI_PROJECT_ID"`) then a shared script that creates the service if missing, builds the app with `oc new-app . --name=$APP --strategy=docker`, starts the build with `oc start-build $APP --from-dir=. --follow || sleep 3s && oc start-build $APP --from-dir=. --follow`, and exposes the route with `oc expose service $APP --hostname=$APP_HOST`; it runs only on master, is manual, sets environment production with URL `http://$CI_PROJECT_NAME.$OPENSHIFT_DOMAIN` and variables APP=production, APP_HOST=$CI_PROJECT_NAME.$OPENSHIFT_DOMAIN. Add a review job in the review stage using the same before_script and script, environment review/$CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME (on_stop stop-review) with URL `http://$CI_PROJECT_NAME-$CI_ENVIRONMENT_SLUG.$OPENSHIFT_DOMAIN`, runs on all branches except master, and sets APP=review-$CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME and APP_HOST accordingly. Add a staging job in the staging stage similar to production but with APP=staging and URL `http://$CI_PROJECT_NAME-staging.$OPENSHIFT_DOMAIN` (only on master), and a stop-review job in the cleanup stage that runs the shared before_script then `oc delete all -l "app=$APP"`, stops the review environment, runs on branches except master, is manual, and sets APP=review-$CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME and GIT_STRATEGY=none.
